revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Tosa-jinja]][[en:Tosa Shrine]][[eu:Tosa jinja]][[fr:Tosa-jinja]][[ja:土佐神社]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]][[zh:土佐神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q704890]] Wikidata name: Tosa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kochi, Kochi, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tosa Jinja
土佐神社 | image = File:Tosa-jinja haiden-4.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Tosa Jinja {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Kochi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.5925|N|133.57694444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Tosa Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610}} | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 16-1 Ikkushinane 2-chōme, Kōchi-shi, Kōchi-ken 781-8131 | website = {{Official website|1=https://tosajinja.com}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = March 11–13 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251}} }} {{nihongo|''''Tosa jinja'''|土佐神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya-shinane neighborhood in the northeastern part of the city of [[:en:Kochi, Japan|Kochi, Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' ].[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually from March 11–13.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} Several of the shrine structures are designated National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]. The shrine's Shinane Festival, held on August 25, is known as one of the three major festivals of Kochi. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tosa Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ajisukitakahikone]]'''| 味鋤高彦根神|}}, the son of [[Ōkuninushi]] and ancestor of the Kano clan, the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of Tosa Province; also called Kano Omikami * {{nihongo|'''Hitokotonushu no kami'''|一言主神|}}, who appears in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' in the section of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]] as an oracle god from Mount Yamato Katsuragi ==History== It is not known when the Tosa Shrine was founded. Per the shrine's legend (as recounted in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] ''[[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]]'') when [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]] was hunting near [[:en:Mount Yamato Katsuragi|Mount Yamato Katsuragi]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], he encountered Hitokoto-no-nushi no kami, but due to his irreverent behavior the Emperor had him exiled to [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]]. The exiled deity was first enshrined at a place called 'Kamonochi' where the locals worshipped a god called "Kano Omikami" and was later transferred to the present location. There are various candidates vying for the location of this first shrine, including the Kano Jinja in the town of [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio]], Kano Jinja in [[:en:Susaki, Kōchi|Susaki]] and Narunashi Jinja in Susaki. The exact location of the Tosa [[Kokufu]] is also uncertain; however, the area around the shrine has many late [[Kofun period]] (6th century to 7th century) [[Kofun]]s and per the "Tosa no Kuni Fudoki", there was a government administrative complex located some two kilometers east of the Tosa Shrine. The first historical record of Tosa Shrine is found in "[[Nihon Shoki]]" in an entry dated to the 4th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] (675), which details the submission of the local Tsuchisa no Ōkami Hosai clan to the Imperial Court. In addition, an entry of August in 686 mentions the dispatch of offerings from the Imperial Court to Tsuchiza no Ōkami following the [[:en:684 Hakuhō earthquake|Hakuho Earthquake]] of 684, and response to Emperor Tenmu's deteriorating physical condition from June 686. In the "Shinsho Kakuchokufusho" of 765, 20 of the 53 shrines added to 'Takakamo no kami' were found in Tosa Province, suggesting a relationship with the [[Kamo clan]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The Tosa Shrine was promoted to the highest Rank in 940 AD due to successful prayers for the suppression of the [[:en:Fujiwara no Sumitomo|Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] rebellion. In the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records, the shrine was named as a ''Myōjin-taisha'', the only such shrine in Tosa Province. Per the "Hyakurensho" records of 1224, the shrine was completely destroyed by a [[:en:Typhoon|Typhoon]]. The shrine is first named as the ''ichinomiya'' of they province in 1326 in an account of a battle between forces of the [[:en:Northern Court|Northern]] and [[Southern Court]]s which occurred in the vicinity. It is believed that a ''[[shōen]]'' manor called Ichinomiya-shō was formed around the shrine during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and that the priests of the shrine became secularized samurai in the Muromachi period under the name of the "Ichinomiya clan". The shrine was destroyed again around 1509 in the wars of the early [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]],and was reconstructed by [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]], with the current [[Honden]], [[Heiden (Shinto)|Heiden]] and [[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] of the shrine completed in 1571. Various vassals of the Chōsokabe were assigned to reconstruct lesser structures and several villages in the vicinity of the shrine were assigned to the shrine for its upkeep. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], the [[:en:Yamauchi clan|Yamauchi clan]] who ruled as [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] of [[:en:Tosa Domain|Tosa Domain]], further constructed to the shrine, with the second ''daimyō'', Yamauchi Tadayoshi, contributing the ''[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]]'' tower gate in 1631 and a drum tower in 1649. Following the Meiji restoration, the shrine was renamed Tosa Jinja in 1871 and it was designated a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|''Kokuhei-chusha''}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], it was redesignated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines|Jinja Honchō]]. In 2003, an [[archaeological excavation]] on the west side of the precincts found the foundations of the medieval mansion and other structures related to temples and shrines. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *{{nihongo|'''Honden'''|本殿||}}, built by Chōsokabe Motochika, and completed in 1571. It is a five by four bay structure with a gabled and thatched roof. The exterior is painted in rich colors and carved everywhere, and the interior is divided into inner and outer sanctuaries.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3414|title= 土佐神社本殿、幣殿及び拝殿|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Honden, Heiden oyobi Haiden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *{{nihongo|'''Heiden'''|幣殿||}} and {{nihongo|'''Haiden'''|拝殿||}}, also built by Chōsokabe Motochika, and completed in 1571. The buildings are arranged so as to represent the shape of a dragonfly flying into the main shrine, and this layout unique to Tosa Shrine. It is said to symbolize a triumphant return from battle. *{{nihongo|'''Drum Tower'''|鼓楼|| Korō}},located southeast of the Heiden. It was built in 1649 in the early Edo period by Yamauchi Tadayoshi. It is a two-story structure with a gabled and thatched roof. The upper floor has carvings and pillars painted with colors, and a drum is hung inside to tell the time.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3416|title=土佐神社鼓楼|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *{{nihongo|'''Rōmon'''|楼門 ||}}, also known as the 'Shinkomon,' it was built by Yamauchi Tadayoshi in 1631 in the early Edo period. It is a two-storied gate with three girder rows and two spans between beams (a two-storied gate with no eaves between the first and upper floors), and the roof is gabled and covered with copper. The first floor enshrines they [[:en:Zuijin|Zuijin]] between the left and right sides. The upper floor is surrounded by balustrades. The whole building is made of bare wood with almost no decoration.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3417|title=土佐神社楼門|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} Tosa-jinja honden.JPG|Honden Tosa-jinja haiden-4.jpg|Haiden Tosa-jinja10s3872.jpg|Heiden Tosa-jinja korou-3.jpg|Drum Tower Tosazinzya01.jpg|Romon ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==Notes== {{reflist|1}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). [https://books.google.com/books?id=_6rXAAAAMAAJ&q=Visiting+famous+shrines+in+japan ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{commons Category-inline|Tosa-jinja}} *[https://tosajinja.com Official home page] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] }} [[en:Tosa Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tosa-jinja}} [[eu:Tosa jinja]] [[fr:Tosa-jinja]] [[ja:土佐神社]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]] [[zh:土佐神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

(1964). [https://books.google.com/books?id=_6rXAAAAMAAJ&q=Visiting+famous+shrines+in+japan ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{commons Category-inline|Tosa-jinja}} *[https://tosajinja.com Official home page] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] }} [[en:Tosa Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tosa-jinja}} [[eu:Tosa jinja]] [[fr:Tosa-jinja]] [[ja:土佐神社]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]] [[zh:土佐神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

土佐神社 | image = File:Tosa-jinja haiden-4.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Tosa Jinja {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Kochi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.5925|N|133.57694444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Tosa Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610}} | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 16-1 Ikkushinane 2-chōme, Kōchi-shi, Kōchi-ken 781-8131 | website = {{Official website|1=https://tosajinja.com}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = March 11–13 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251}} }} {{nihongo|''''Tosa jinja'''|土佐神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya-shinane neighborhood in the northeastern part of the city of [[:en:Kochi, Japan|Kochi, Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' ].[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually from March 11–13.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} Several of the shrine structures are designated National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]. The shrine's Shinane Festival, held on August 25, is known as one of the three major festivals of Kochi. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tosa Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ajisukitakahikone]]'''| 味鋤高彦根神|}}, the son of [[Ōkuninushi]] and ancestor of the Kano clan, the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of Tosa Province; also called Kano Omikami * {{nihongo|'''Hitokotonushu no kami'''|一言主神|}}, who appears in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' in the section of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]] as an oracle god from Mount Yamato Katsuragi ==History== It is not known when the Tosa Shrine was founded. Per the shrine's legend (as recounted in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] ''[[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]]'') when [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]] was hunting near [[:en:Mount Yamato Katsuragi|Mount Yamato Katsuragi]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], he encountered Hitokoto-no-nushi no kami, but due to his irreverent behavior the Emperor had him exiled to [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]]. The exiled deity was first enshrined at a place called 'Kamonochi' where the locals worshipped a god called "Kano Omikami" and was later transferred to the present location. There are various candidates vying for the location of this first shrine, including the Kano Jinja in the town of [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio]], Kano Jinja in [[:en:Susaki, Kōchi|Susaki]] and Narunashi Jinja in Susaki. The exact location of the Tosa [[Kokufu]] is also uncertain; however, the area around the shrine has many late [[Kofun period]] (6th century to 7th century) [[Kofun]]s and per the "Tosa no Kuni Fudoki", there was a government administrative complex located some two kilometers east of the Tosa Shrine. The first historical record of Tosa Shrine is found in "[[Nihon Shoki]]" in an entry dated to the 4th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] (675), which details the submission of the local Tsuchisa no Ōkami Hosai clan to the Imperial Court. In addition, an entry of August in 686 mentions the dispatch of offerings from the Imperial Court to Tsuchiza no Ōkami following the [[:en:684 Hakuhō earthquake|Hakuho Earthquake]] of 684, and response to Emperor Tenmu's deteriorating physical condition from June 686. In the "Shinsho Kakuchokufusho" of 765, 20 of the 53 shrines added to 'Takakamo no kami' were found in Tosa Province, suggesting a relationship with the [[Kamo clan]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The Tosa Shrine was promoted to the highest Rank in 940 AD due to successful prayers for the suppression of the [[:en:Fujiwara no Sumitomo|Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] rebellion. In the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records, the shrine was named as a ''Myōjin-taisha'', the only such shrine in Tosa Province. Per the "Hyakurensho" records of 1224, the shrine was completely destroyed by a [[:en:Typhoon|Typhoon]]. The shrine is first named as the ''ichinomiya'' of they province in 1326 in an account of a battle between forces of the [[:en:Northern Court|Northern]] and [[Southern Court]]s which occurred in the vicinity. It is believed that a ''[[shōen]]'' manor called Ichinomiya-shō was formed around the shrine during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and that the priests of the shrine became secularized samurai in the Muromachi period under the name of the "Ichinomiya clan". The shrine was destroyed again around 1509 in the wars of the early [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]],and was reconstructed by [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]], with the current [[Honden]], [[Heiden (Shinto)|Heiden]] and [[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] of the shrine completed in 1571. Various vassals of the Chōsokabe were assigned to reconstruct lesser structures and several villages in the vicinity of the shrine were assigned to the shrine for its upkeep. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], the [[:en:Yamauchi clan|Yamauchi clan]] who ruled as [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] of [[:en:Tosa Domain|Tosa Domain]], further constructed to the shrine, with the second ''daimyō'', Yamauchi Tadayoshi, contributing the ''[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]]'' tower gate in 1631 and a drum tower in 1649. Following the Meiji restoration, the shrine was renamed Tosa Jinja in 1871 and it was designated a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|''Kokuhei-chusha''}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], it was redesignated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines|Jinja Honchō]]. In 2003, an [[archaeological excavation]] on the west side of the precincts found the foundations of the medieval mansion and other structures related to temples and shrines. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *{{nihongo|'''Honden'''|本殿||}}, built by Chōsokabe Motochika, and completed in 1571. It is a five by four bay structure with a gabled and thatched roof. The exterior is painted in rich colors and carved everywhere, and the interior is divided into inner and outer sanctuaries.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3414|title= 土佐神社本殿、幣殿及び拝殿|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Honden, Heiden oyobi Haiden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *{{nihongo|'''Heiden'''|幣殿||}} and {{nihongo|'''Haiden'''|拝殿||}}, also built by Chōsokabe Motochika, and completed in 1571. The buildings are arranged so as to represent the shape of a dragonfly flying into the main shrine, and this layout unique to Tosa Shrine. It is said to symbolize a triumphant return from battle. *{{nihongo|'''Drum Tower'''|鼓楼|| Korō}},located southeast of the Heiden. It was built in 1649 in the early Edo period by Yamauchi Tadayoshi. It is a two-story structure with a gabled and thatched roof. The upper floor has carvings and pillars painted with colors, and a drum is hung inside to tell the time.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3416|title=土佐神社鼓楼|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *{{nihongo|'''Rōmon'''|楼門 ||}}, also known as the 'Shinkomon,' it was built by Yamauchi Tadayoshi in 1631 in the early Edo period. It is a two-storied gate with three girder rows and two spans between beams (a two-storied gate with no eaves between the first and upper floors), and the roof is gabled and covered with copper. The first floor enshrines they [[:en:Zuijin|Zuijin]] between the left and right sides. The upper floor is surrounded by balustrades. The whole building is made of bare wood with almost no decoration.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3417|title=土佐神社楼門|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} Tosa-jinja honden.JPG|Honden Tosa-jinja haiden-4.jpg|Haiden Tosa-jinja10s3872.jpg|Heiden Tosa-jinja korou-3.jpg|Drum Tower Tosazinzya01.jpg|Romon ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==Notes== {{reflist|1}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). [https://books.google.com/books?id=_6rXAAAAMAAJ&q=Visiting+famous+shrines+in+japan ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{commons Category-inline|Tosa-jinja}} *[https://tosajinja.com Official home page] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] }} [[en:Tosa Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tosa-jinja}} [[eu:Tosa jinja]] [[fr:Tosa-jinja]] [[ja:土佐神社]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]] [[zh:土佐神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Following the Meiji restoration, the shrine was renamed Tosa Jinja in 1871 and it was designated a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|''Kokuhei-chusha''}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], it was redesignated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines|Jinja Honchō]]. In 2003, an [[archaeological excavation]] on the west side of the precincts found the foundations of the medieval mansion and other structures related to temples and shrines. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *{{nihongo|'''Honden'''|本殿||}}, built by Chōsokabe Motochika, and completed in 1571. It is a five by four bay structure with a gabled and thatched roof. The exterior is painted in rich colors and carved everywhere, and the interior is divided into inner and outer sanctuaries.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3414|title= 土佐神社本殿、幣殿及び拝殿|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Honden, Heiden oyobi Haiden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *{{nihongo|'''Heiden'''|幣殿||}} and {{nihongo|'''Haiden'''|拝殿||}}, also built by Chōsokabe Motochika, and completed in 1571. The buildings are arranged so as to represent the shape of a dragonfly flying into the main shrine, and this layout unique to Tosa Shrine. It is said to symbolize a triumphant return from battle. *{{nihongo|'''Drum Tower'''|鼓楼|| Korō}},located southeast of the Heiden. It was built in 1649 in the early Edo period by Yamauchi Tadayoshi. It is a two-story structure with a gabled and thatched roof. The upper floor has carvings and pillars painted with colors, and a drum is hung inside to tell the time.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3416|title=土佐神社鼓楼|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *{{nihongo|'''Rōmon'''|楼門 ||}}, also known as the 'Shinkomon,' it was built by Yamauchi Tadayoshi in 1631 in the early Edo period. It is a two-storied gate with three girder rows and two spans between beams (a two-storied gate with no eaves between the first and upper floors), and the roof is gabled and covered with copper. The first floor enshrines they [[:en:Zuijin|Zuijin]] between the left and right sides. The upper floor is surrounded by balustrades. The whole building is made of bare wood with almost no decoration.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3417|title=土佐神社楼門|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} Tosa-jinja honden.JPG|Honden Tosa-jinja haiden-4.jpg|Haiden Tosa-jinja10s3872.jpg|Heiden Tosa-jinja korou-3.jpg|Drum Tower Tosazinzya01.jpg|Romon ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==Notes== {{reflist|1}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). [https://books.google.com/books?id=_6rXAAAAMAAJ&q=Visiting+famous+shrines+in+japan ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{commons Category-inline|Tosa-jinja}} *[https://tosajinja.com Official home page] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] }} [[en:Tosa Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tosa-jinja}} [[eu:Tosa jinja]] [[fr:Tosa-jinja]] [[ja:土佐神社]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]] [[zh:土佐神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Tosa-jinja]][[en:Tosa Shrine]][[eu:Tosa jinja]][[fr:Tosa-jinja]][[ja:土佐神社]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]][[zh:土佐神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q704890]] Wikidata name: Tosa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kochi, Kochi, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tosa Jinja
土佐神社 | image = File:Tosa-jinja haiden-4.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Tosa Jinja {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Kochi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.5925|N|133.57694444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Tosa Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610}} | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 16-1 Ikkushinane 2-chōme, Kōchi-shi, Kōchi-ken 781-8131 | website = {{Official website|1=https://tosajinja.com}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = March 11–13 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251}} }} {{nihongo|''''Tosa jinja'''|土佐神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya-shinane neighborhood in the northeastern part of the city of [[:en:Kochi, Japan|Kochi, Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' ].[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually from March 11–13.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} Several of the shrine structures are designated National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]. The shrine's Shinane Festival, held on August 25, is known as one of the three major festivals of Kochi. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tosa Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ajisukitakahikone]]'''| 味鋤高彦根神|}}, the son of [[Ōkuninushi]] and ancestor of the Kano clan, the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of Tosa Province; also called Kano Omikami * {{nihongo|'''Hitokotonushu no kami'''|一言主神|}}, who appears in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' in the section of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]] as an oracle god from Mount Yamato Katsuragi ==History== It is not known when the Tosa Shrine was founded. Per the shrine's legend (as recounted in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] ''[[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]]'') when [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]] was hunting near [[:en:Mount Yamato Katsuragi|Mount Yamato Katsuragi]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], he encountered Hitokoto-no-nushi no kami, but due to his irreverent behavior the Emperor had him exiled to [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]]. The exiled deity was first enshrined at a place called 'Kamonochi' where the locals worshipped a god called "Kano Omikami" and was later transferred to the present location. There are various candidates vying for the location of this first shrine, including the Kano Jinja in the town of [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio]], Kano Jinja in [[:en:Susaki, Kōchi|Susaki]] and Narunashi Jinja in Susaki. The exact location of the Tosa [[Kokufu]] is also uncertain; however, the area around the shrine has many late [[Kofun period]] (6th century to 7th century) [[Kofun]]s and per the "Tosa no Kuni Fudoki", there was a government administrative complex located some two kilometers east of the Tosa Shrine. The first historical record of Tosa Shrine is found in "[[Nihon Shoki]]" in an entry dated to the 4th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] (675), which details the submission of the local Tsuchisa no Ōkami Hosai clan to the Imperial Court. In addition, an entry of August in 686 mentions the dispatch of offerings from the Imperial Court to Tsuchiza no Ōkami following the [[:en:684 Hakuhō earthquake|Hakuho Earthquake]] of 684, and response to Emperor Tenmu's deteriorating physical condition from June 686. In the "Shinsho Kakuchokufusho" of 765, 20 of the 53 shrines added to 'Takakamo no kami' were found in Tosa Province, suggesting a relationship with the [[Kamo clan]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The Tosa Shrine was promoted to the highest Rank in 940 AD due to successful prayers for the suppression of the [[:en:Fujiwara no Sumitomo|Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] rebellion. In the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records, the shrine was named as a ''Myōjin-taisha'', the only such shrine in Tosa Province. Per the "Hyakurensho" records of 1224, the shrine was completely destroyed by a [[:en:Typhoon|Typhoon]]. The shrine is first named as the ''ichinomiya'' of they province in 1326 in an account of a battle between forces of the [[:en:Northern Court|Northern]] and [[Southern Court]]s which occurred in the vicinity. It is believed that a ''[[shōen]]'' manor called Ichinomiya-shō was formed around the shrine during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and that the priests of the shrine became secularized samurai in the Muromachi period under the name of the "Ichinomiya clan". The shrine was destroyed again around 1509 in the wars of the early [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]],and was reconstructed by [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]], with the current [[Honden]], [[Heiden (Shinto)|Heiden]] and [[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] of the shrine completed in 1571. Various vassals of the Chōsokabe were assigned to reconstruct lesser structures and several villages in the vicinity of the shrine were assigned to the shrine for its upkeep. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], the [[:en:Yamauchi clan|Yamauchi clan]] who ruled as [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] of [[:en:Tosa Domain|Tosa Domain]], further constructed to the shrine, with the second ''daimyō'', Yamauchi Tadayoshi, contributing the ''[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]]'' tower gate in 1631 and a drum tower in 1649. Following the Meiji restoration, the shrine was renamed Tosa Jinja in 1871 and it was designated a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|''Kokuhei-chusha''}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], it was redesignated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines|Jinja Honchō]]. In 2003, an [[archaeological excavation]] on the west side of the precincts found the foundations of the medieval mansion and other structures related to temples and shrines. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *{{nihongo|'''Honden'''|本殿||}}, built by Chōsokabe Motochika, and completed in 1571. It is a five by four bay structure with a gabled and thatched roof. The exterior is painted in rich colors and carved everywhere, and the interior is divided into inner and outer sanctuaries.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3414|title= 土佐神社本殿、幣殿及び拝殿|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Honden, Heiden oyobi Haiden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *{{nihongo|'''Heiden'''|幣殿||}} and {{nihongo|'''Haiden'''|拝殿||}}, also built by Chōsokabe Motochika, and completed in 1571. The buildings are arranged so as to represent the shape of a dragonfly flying into the main shrine, and this layout unique to Tosa Shrine. It is said to symbolize a triumphant return from battle. *{{nihongo|'''Drum Tower'''|鼓楼|| Korō}},located southeast of the Heiden. It was built in 1649 in the early Edo period by Yamauchi Tadayoshi. It is a two-story structure with a gabled and thatched roof. The upper floor has carvings and pillars painted with colors, and a drum is hung inside to tell the time.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3416|title=土佐神社鼓楼|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *{{nihongo|'''Rōmon'''|楼門 ||}}, also known as the 'Shinkomon,' it was built by Yamauchi Tadayoshi in 1631 in the early Edo period. It is a two-storied gate with three girder rows and two spans between beams (a two-storied gate with no eaves between the first and upper floors), and the roof is gabled and covered with copper. The first floor enshrines they [[:en:Zuijin|Zuijin]] between the left and right sides. The upper floor is surrounded by balustrades. The whole building is made of bare wood with almost no decoration.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3417|title=土佐神社楼門|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} Tosa-jinja honden.JPG|Honden Tosa-jinja haiden-4.jpg|Haiden Tosa-jinja10s3872.jpg|Heiden Tosa-jinja korou-3.jpg|Drum Tower Tosazinzya01.jpg|Romon ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==Notes== {{reflist|1}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). [https://books.google.com/books?id=_6rXAAAAMAAJ&q=Visiting+famous+shrines+in+japan ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{commons Category-inline|Tosa-jinja}} *[https://tosajinja.com Official home page] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] }} [[en:Tosa Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tosa-jinja}} [[eu:Tosa jinja]] [[fr:Tosa-jinja]] [[ja:土佐神社]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]] [[zh:土佐神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

The whole building is made of bare wood with almost no decoration.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3417|title=土佐神社楼門|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} Tosa-jinja honden.JPG|Honden Tosa-jinja haiden-4.jpg|Haiden Tosa-jinja10s3872.jpg|Heiden Tosa-jinja korou-3.jpg|Drum Tower Tosazinzya01.jpg|Romon ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==Notes== {{reflist|1}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). [https://books.google.com/books?id=_6rXAAAAMAAJ&q=Visiting+famous+shrines+in+japan ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{commons Category-inline|Tosa-jinja}} *[https://tosajinja.com Official home page] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] }} [[en:Tosa Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tosa-jinja}} [[eu:Tosa jinja]] [[fr:Tosa-jinja]] [[ja:土佐神社]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]] [[zh:土佐神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Tosa-jinja]][[en:Tosa Shrine]][[eu:Tosa jinja]][[fr:Tosa-jinja]][[ja:土佐神社]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]][[zh:土佐神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q704890]] Wikidata name: Tosa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kochi, Kochi, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tosa Jinja
土佐神社 | image = File:Tosa-jinja haiden-4.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Tosa Jinja {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Kochi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.5925|N|133.57694444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Tosa Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610}} | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 16-1 Ikkushinane 2-chōme, Kōchi-shi, Kōchi-ken 781-8131 | website = {{Official website|1=https://tosajinja.com}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = March 11–13 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251}} }} {{nihongo|''''Tosa jinja'''|土佐神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya-shinane neighborhood in the northeastern part of the city of [[:en:Kochi, Japan|Kochi, Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' ].[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually from March 11–13.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} Several of the shrine structures are designated National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]. The shrine's Shinane Festival, held on August 25, is known as one of the three major festivals of Kochi. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tosa Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ajisukitakahikone]]'''| 味鋤高彦根神|}}, the son of [[Ōkuninushi]] and ancestor of the Kano clan, the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of Tosa Province; also called Kano Omikami * {{nihongo|'''Hitokotonushu no kami'''|一言主神|}}, who appears in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' in the section of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]] as an oracle god from Mount Yamato Katsuragi ==History== It is not known when the Tosa Shrine was founded. Per the shrine's legend (as recounted in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] ''[[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]]'') when [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]] was hunting near [[:en:Mount Yamato Katsuragi|Mount Yamato Katsuragi]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], he encountered Hitokoto-no-nushi no kami, but due to his irreverent behavior the Emperor had him exiled to [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]]. The exiled deity was first enshrined at a place called 'Kamonochi' where the locals worshipped a god called "Kano Omikami" and was later transferred to the present location. There are various candidates vying for the location of this first shrine, including the Kano Jinja in the town of [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio]], Kano Jinja in [[:en:Susaki, Kōchi|Susaki]] and Narunashi Jinja in Susaki. The exact location of the Tosa [[Kokufu]] is also uncertain; however, the area around the shrine has many late [[Kofun period]] (6th century to 7th century) [[Kofun]]s and per the "Tosa no Kuni Fudoki", there was a government administrative complex located some two kilometers east of the Tosa Shrine. The first historical record of Tosa Shrine is found in "[[Nihon Shoki]]" in an entry dated to the 4th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] (675), which details the submission of the local Tsuchisa no Ōkami Hosai clan to the Imperial Court. In addition, an entry of August in 686 mentions the dispatch of offerings from the Imperial Court to Tsuchiza no Ōkami following the [[:en:684 Hakuhō earthquake|Hakuho Earthquake]] of 684, and response to Emperor Tenmu's deteriorating physical condition from June 686. In the "Shinsho Kakuchokufusho" of 765, 20 of the 53 shrines added to 'Takakamo no kami' were found in Tosa Province, suggesting a relationship with the [[Kamo clan]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The Tosa Shrine was promoted to the highest Rank in 940 AD due to successful prayers for the suppression of the [[:en:Fujiwara no Sumitomo|Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] rebellion. In the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records, the shrine was named as a ''Myōjin-taisha'', the only such shrine in Tosa Province. Per the "Hyakurensho" records of 1224, the shrine was completely destroyed by a [[:en:Typhoon|Typhoon]]. The shrine is first named as the ''ichinomiya'' of they province in 1326 in an account of a battle between forces of the [[:en:Northern Court|Northern]] and [[Southern Court]]s which occurred in the vicinity. It is believed that a ''[[shōen]]'' manor called Ichinomiya-shō was formed around the shrine during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and that the priests of the shrine became secularized samurai in the Muromachi period under the name of the "Ichinomiya clan". The shrine was destroyed again around 1509 in the wars of the early [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]],and was reconstructed by [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]], with the current [[Honden]], [[Heiden (Shinto)|Heiden]] and [[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] of the shrine completed in 1571. Various vassals of the Chōsokabe were assigned to reconstruct lesser structures and several villages in the vicinity of the shrine were assigned to the shrine for its upkeep. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], the [[:en:Yamauchi clan|Yamauchi clan]] who ruled as [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] of [[:en:Tosa Domain|Tosa Domain]], further constructed to the shrine, with the second ''daimyō'', Yamauchi Tadayoshi, contributing the ''[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]]'' tower gate in 1631 and a drum tower in 1649. Following the Meiji restoration, the shrine was renamed Tosa Jinja in 1871 and it was designated a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|''Kokuhei-chusha''}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], it was redesignated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines|Jinja Honchō]]. In 2003, an [[archaeological excavation]] on the west side of the precincts found the foundations of the medieval mansion and other structures related to temples and shrines. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *{{nihongo|'''Honden'''|本殿||}}, built by Chōsokabe Motochika, and completed in 1571. It is a five by four bay structure with a gabled and thatched roof. The exterior is painted in rich colors and carved everywhere, and the interior is divided into inner and outer sanctuaries.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3414|title= 土佐神社本殿、幣殿及び拝殿|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Honden, Heiden oyobi Haiden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *{{nihongo|'''Heiden'''|幣殿||}} and {{nihongo|'''Haiden'''|拝殿||}}, also built by Chōsokabe Motochika, and completed in 1571. The buildings are arranged so as to represent the shape of a dragonfly flying into the main shrine, and this layout unique to Tosa Shrine. It is said to symbolize a triumphant return from battle. *{{nihongo|'''Drum Tower'''|鼓楼|| Korō}},located southeast of the Heiden. It was built in 1649 in the early Edo period by Yamauchi Tadayoshi. It is a two-story structure with a gabled and thatched roof. The upper floor has carvings and pillars painted with colors, and a drum is hung inside to tell the time.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3416|title=土佐神社鼓楼|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *{{nihongo|'''Rōmon'''|楼門 ||}}, also known as the 'Shinkomon,' it was built by Yamauchi Tadayoshi in 1631 in the early Edo period. It is a two-storied gate with three girder rows and two spans between beams (a two-storied gate with no eaves between the first and upper floors), and the roof is gabled and covered with copper. The first floor enshrines they [[:en:Zuijin|Zuijin]] between the left and right sides. The upper floor is surrounded by balustrades. The whole building is made of bare wood with almost no decoration.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3417|title=土佐神社楼門|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} Tosa-jinja honden.JPG|Honden Tosa-jinja haiden-4.jpg|Haiden Tosa-jinja10s3872.jpg|Heiden Tosa-jinja korou-3.jpg|Drum Tower Tosazinzya01.jpg|Romon ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==Notes== {{reflist|1}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). [https://books.google.com/books?id=_6rXAAAAMAAJ&q=Visiting+famous+shrines+in+japan ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{commons Category-inline|Tosa-jinja}} *[https://tosajinja.com Official home page] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] }} [[en:Tosa Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tosa-jinja}} [[eu:Tosa jinja]] [[fr:Tosa-jinja]] [[ja:土佐神社]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]] [[zh:土佐神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

(1964). [https://books.google.com/books?id=_6rXAAAAMAAJ&q=Visiting+famous+shrines+in+japan ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{commons Category-inline|Tosa-jinja}} *[https://tosajinja.com Official home page] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] }} [[en:Tosa Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tosa-jinja}} [[eu:Tosa jinja]] [[fr:Tosa-jinja]] [[ja:土佐神社]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]] [[zh:土佐神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}}

土佐神社 | image = File:Tosa-jinja haiden-4.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Tosa Jinja {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Kochi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.5925|N|133.57694444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Tosa Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610}} | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 16-1 Ikkushinane 2-chōme, Kōchi-shi, Kōchi-ken 781-8131 | website = {{Official website|1=https://tosajinja.com}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = March 11–13 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251}} }} {{nihongo|''''Tosa jinja'''|土佐神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya-shinane neighborhood in the northeastern part of the city of [[:en:Kochi, Japan|Kochi, Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' ].[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually from March 11–13.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} Several of the shrine structures are designated National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]. The shrine's Shinane Festival, held on August 25, is known as one of the three major festivals of Kochi. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tosa Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ajisukitakahikone]]'''| 味鋤高彦根神|}}, the son of [[Ōkuninushi]] and ancestor of the Kano clan, the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of Tosa Province; also called Kano Omikami * {{nihongo|'''Hitokotonushu no kami'''|一言主神|}}, who appears in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' in the section of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]] as an oracle god from Mount Yamato Katsuragi ==History== It is not known when the Tosa Shrine was founded. Per the shrine's legend (as recounted in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] ''[[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]]'') when [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]] was hunting near [[:en:Mount Yamato Katsuragi|Mount Yamato Katsuragi]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], he encountered Hitokoto-no-nushi no kami, but due to his irreverent behavior the Emperor had him exiled to [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]]. The exiled deity was first enshrined at a place called 'Kamonochi' where the locals worshipped a god called "Kano Omikami" and was later transferred to the present location. There are various candidates vying for the location of this first shrine, including the Kano Jinja in the town of [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio]], Kano Jinja in [[:en:Susaki, Kōchi|Susaki]] and Narunashi Jinja in Susaki. The exact location of the Tosa [[Kokufu]] is also uncertain; however, the area around the shrine has many late [[Kofun period]] (6th century to 7th century) [[Kofun]]s and per the "Tosa no Kuni Fudoki", there was a government administrative complex located some two kilometers east of the Tosa Shrine. The first historical record of Tosa Shrine is found in "[[Nihon Shoki]]" in an entry dated to the 4th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] (675), which details the submission of the local Tsuchisa no Ōkami Hosai clan to the Imperial Court. In addition, an entry of August in 686 mentions the dispatch of offerings from the Imperial Court to Tsuchiza no Ōkami following the [[:en:684 Hakuhō earthquake|Hakuho Earthquake]] of 684, and response to Emperor Tenmu's deteriorating physical condition from June 686. In the "Shinsho Kakuchokufusho" of 765, 20 of the 53 shrines added to 'Takakamo no kami' were found in Tosa Province, suggesting a relationship with the [[Kamo clan]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The Tosa Shrine was promoted to the highest Rank in 940 AD due to successful prayers for the suppression of the [[:en:Fujiwara no Sumitomo|Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] rebellion. In the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records, the shrine was named as a ''Myōjin-taisha'', the only such shrine in Tosa Province. Per the "Hyakurensho" records of 1224, the shrine was completely destroyed by a [[:en:Typhoon|Typhoon]]. The shrine is first named as the ''ichinomiya'' of they province in 1326 in an account of a battle between forces of the [[:en:Northern Court|Northern]] and [[Southern Court]]s which occurred in the vicinity. It is believed that a ''[[shōen]]'' manor called Ichinomiya-shō was formed around the shrine during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and that the priests of the shrine became secularized samurai in the Muromachi period under the name of the "Ichinomiya clan". The shrine was destroyed again around 1509 in the wars of the early [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]],and was reconstructed by [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]], with the current [[Honden]], [[Heiden (Shinto)|Heiden]] and [[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] of the shrine completed in 1571. Various vassals of the Chōsokabe were assigned to reconstruct lesser structures and several villages in the vicinity of the shrine were assigned to the shrine for its upkeep. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], the [[:en:Yamauchi clan|Yamauchi clan]] who ruled as [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] of [[:en:Tosa Domain|Tosa Domain]], further constructed to the shrine, with the second ''daimyō'', Yamauchi Tadayoshi, contributing the ''[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]]'' tower gate in 1631 and a drum tower in 1649. Following the Meiji restoration, the shrine was renamed Tosa Jinja in 1871 and it was designated a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|''Kokuhei-chusha''}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], it was redesignated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines|Jinja Honchō]]. In 2003, an [[archaeological excavation]] on the west side of the precincts found the foundations of the medieval mansion and other structures related to temples and shrines. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *{{nihongo|'''Honden'''|本殿||}}, built by Chōsokabe Motochika, and completed in 1571. It is a five by four bay structure with a gabled and thatched roof. The exterior is painted in rich colors and carved everywhere, and the interior is divided into inner and outer sanctuaries.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3414|title= 土佐神社本殿、幣殿及び拝殿|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Honden, Heiden oyobi Haiden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *{{nihongo|'''Heiden'''|幣殿||}} and {{nihongo|'''Haiden'''|拝殿||}}, also built by Chōsokabe Motochika, and completed in 1571. The buildings are arranged so as to represent the shape of a dragonfly flying into the main shrine, and this layout unique to Tosa Shrine. It is said to symbolize a triumphant return from battle. *{{nihongo|'''Drum Tower'''|鼓楼|| Korō}},located southeast of the Heiden. It was built in 1649 in the early Edo period by Yamauchi Tadayoshi. It is a two-story structure with a gabled and thatched roof. The upper floor has carvings and pillars painted with colors, and a drum is hung inside to tell the time.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3416|title=土佐神社鼓楼|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *{{nihongo|'''Rōmon'''|楼門 ||}}, also known as the 'Shinkomon,' it was built by Yamauchi Tadayoshi in 1631 in the early Edo period. It is a two-storied gate with three girder rows and two spans between beams (a two-storied gate with no eaves between the first and upper floors), and the roof is gabled and covered with copper. The first floor enshrines they [[:en:Zuijin|Zuijin]] between the left and right sides. The upper floor is surrounded by balustrades. The whole building is made of bare wood with almost no decoration.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3417|title=土佐神社楼門|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} Tosa-jinja honden.JPG|Honden Tosa-jinja haiden-4.jpg|Haiden Tosa-jinja10s3872.jpg|Heiden Tosa-jinja korou-3.jpg|Drum Tower Tosazinzya01.jpg|Romon ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==Notes== {{reflist|1}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). [https://books.google.com/books?id=_6rXAAAAMAAJ&q=Visiting+famous+shrines+in+japan ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{commons Category-inline|Tosa-jinja}} *[https://tosajinja.com Official home page] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] }} [[en:Tosa Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tosa-jinja}} [[eu:Tosa jinja]] [[fr:Tosa-jinja]] [[ja:土佐神社]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]] [[zh:土佐神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}}

It is believed that a ''[[shōen]]'' manor called Ichinomiya-shō was formed around the shrine during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and that the priests of the shrine became secularized samurai in the Muromachi period under the name of the "Ichinomiya clan". The shrine was destroyed again around 1509 in the wars of the early [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]],and was reconstructed by [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]], with the current [[Honden]], [[Heiden (Shinto)|Heiden]] and [[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] of the shrine completed in 1571. Various vassals of the Chōsokabe were assigned to reconstruct lesser structures and several villages in the vicinity of the shrine were assigned to the shrine for its upkeep. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], the [[:en:Yamauchi clan|Yamauchi clan]] who ruled as [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] of [[:en:Tosa Domain|Tosa Domain]], further constructed to the shrine, with the second ''daimyō'', Yamauchi Tadayoshi, contributing the ''[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]]'' tower gate in 1631 and a drum tower in 1649. Following the Meiji restoration, the shrine was renamed Tosa Jinja in 1871 and it was designated a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|''Kokuhei-chusha''}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], it was redesignated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines|Jinja Honchō]]. In 2003, an [[archaeological excavation]] on the west side of the precincts found the foundations of the medieval mansion and other structures related to temples and shrines. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *{{nihongo|'''Honden'''|本殿||}}, built by Chōsokabe Motochika, and completed in 1571. It is a five by four bay structure with a gabled and thatched roof. The exterior is painted in rich colors and carved everywhere, and the interior is divided into inner and outer sanctuaries.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3414|title= 土佐神社本殿、幣殿及び拝殿|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Honden, Heiden oyobi Haiden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *{{nihongo|'''Heiden'''|幣殿||}} and {{nihongo|'''Haiden'''|拝殿||}}, also built by Chōsokabe Motochika, and completed in 1571. The buildings are arranged so as to represent the shape of a dragonfly flying into the main shrine, and this layout unique to Tosa Shrine. It is said to symbolize a triumphant return from battle. *{{nihongo|'''Drum Tower'''|鼓楼|| Korō}},located southeast of the Heiden. It was built in 1649 in the early Edo period by Yamauchi Tadayoshi. It is a two-story structure with a gabled and thatched roof. The upper floor has carvings and pillars painted with colors, and a drum is hung inside to tell the time.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3416|title=土佐神社鼓楼|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *{{nihongo|'''Rōmon'''|楼門 ||}}, also known as the 'Shinkomon,' it was built by Yamauchi Tadayoshi in 1631 in the early Edo period. It is a two-storied gate with three girder rows and two spans between beams (a two-storied gate with no eaves between the first and upper floors), and the roof is gabled and covered with copper. The first floor enshrines they [[:en:Zuijin|Zuijin]] between the left and right sides. The upper floor is surrounded by balustrades. The whole building is made of bare wood with almost no decoration.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3417|title=土佐神社楼門|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} Tosa-jinja honden.JPG|Honden Tosa-jinja haiden-4.jpg|Haiden Tosa-jinja10s3872.jpg|Heiden Tosa-jinja korou-3.jpg|Drum Tower Tosazinzya01.jpg|Romon ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==Notes== {{reflist|1}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). [https://books.google.com/books?id=_6rXAAAAMAAJ&q=Visiting+famous+shrines+in+japan ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{commons Category-inline|Tosa-jinja}} *[https://tosajinja.com Official home page] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] }} [[en:Tosa Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tosa-jinja}} [[eu:Tosa jinja]] [[fr:Tosa-jinja]] [[ja:土佐神社]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]] [[zh:土佐神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}}

[[commons:Category:Tosa-jinja]][[en:Tosa Shrine]][[eu:Tosa jinja]][[fr:Tosa-jinja]][[ja:土佐神社]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]][[zh:土佐神社]] [[commons:Category:Tosa-jinja]][[en:Tosa Shrine]][[eu:Tosa jinja]][[fr:Tosa-jinja]][[ja:土佐神社]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]][[zh:土佐神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tosa-jinja]][[en:Tosa Shrine]][[eu:Tosa jinja]][[fr:Tosa-jinja]][[ja:土佐神社]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]][[zh:土佐神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q704890]] Wikidata name: Tosa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kochi, Kochi, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tosa Jinja
土佐神社 | image = File:Tosa-jinja haiden-4.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Tosa Jinja {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Kochi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.5925|N|133.57694444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Tosa Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610}} | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 16-1 Ikkushinane 2-chōme, Kōchi-shi, Kōchi-ken 781-8131 | website = {{Official website|1=https://tosajinja.com}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = March 11–13 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251}} }} {{nihongo|''''Tosa jinja'''|土佐神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya-shinane neighborhood in the northeastern part of the city of [[:en:Kochi, Japan|Kochi, Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' ].[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually from March 11–13.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} Several of the shrine structures are designated National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]. The shrine's Shinane Festival, held on August 25, is known as one of the three major festivals of Kochi. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tosa Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ajisukitakahikone]]'''| 味鋤高彦根神|}}, the son of [[Ōkuninushi]] and ancestor of the Kano clan, the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of Tosa Province; also called Kano Omikami * {{nihongo|'''Hitokotonushu no kami'''|一言主神|}}, who appears in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' in the section of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]] as an oracle god from Mount Yamato Katsuragi ==History== It is not known when the Tosa Shrine was founded. Per the shrine's legend (as recounted in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] ''[[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]]'') when [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]] was hunting near [[:en:Mount Yamato Katsuragi|Mount Yamato Katsuragi]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], he encountered Hitokoto-no-nushi no kami, but due to his irreverent behavior the Emperor had him exiled to [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]]. The exiled deity was first enshrined at a place called 'Kamonochi' where the locals worshipped a god called "Kano Omikami" and was later transferred to the present location. There are various candidates vying for the location of this first shrine, including the Kano Jinja in the town of [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio]], Kano Jinja in [[:en:Susaki, Kōchi|Susaki]] and Narunashi Jinja in Susaki. The exact location of the Tosa [[Kokufu]] is also uncertain; however, the area around the shrine has many late [[Kofun period]] (6th century to 7th century) [[Kofun]]s and per the "Tosa no Kuni Fudoki", there was a government administrative complex located some two kilometers east of the Tosa Shrine. The first historical record of Tosa Shrine is found in "[[Nihon Shoki]]" in an entry dated to the 4th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] (675), which details the submission of the local Tsuchisa no Ōkami Hosai clan to the Imperial Court. In addition, an entry of August in 686 mentions the dispatch of offerings from the Imperial Court to Tsuchiza no Ōkami following the [[:en:684 Hakuhō earthquake|Hakuho Earthquake]] of 684, and response to Emperor Tenmu's deteriorating physical condition from June 686. In the "Shinsho Kakuchokufusho" of 765, 20 of the 53 shrines added to 'Takakamo no kami' were found in Tosa Province, suggesting a relationship with the [[Kamo clan]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. The Tosa Shrine was promoted to the highest Rank in 940 AD due to successful prayers for the suppression of the [[:en:Fujiwara no Sumitomo|Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] rebellion. In the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records, the shrine was named as a ''Myōjin-taisha'', the only such shrine in Tosa Province. Per the "Hyakurensho" records of 1224, the shrine was completely destroyed by a [[:en:Typhoon|Typhoon]]. The shrine is first named as the ''ichinomiya'' of they province in 1326 in an account of a battle between forces of the [[:en:Northern Court|Northern]] and [[Southern Court]]s which occurred in the vicinity. It is believed that a ''[[shōen]]'' manor called Ichinomiya-shō was formed around the shrine during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and that the priests of the shrine became secularized samurai in the Muromachi period under the name of the "Ichinomiya clan". The shrine was destroyed again around 1509 in the wars of the early [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]],and was reconstructed by [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]], with the current [[Honden]], [[Heiden (Shinto)|Heiden]] and [[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] of the shrine completed in 1571. Various vassals of the Chōsokabe were assigned to reconstruct lesser structures and several villages in the vicinity of the shrine were assigned to the shrine for its upkeep. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], the [[:en:Yamauchi clan|Yamauchi clan]] who ruled as [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] of [[:en:Tosa Domain|Tosa Domain]], further constructed to the shrine, with the second ''daimyō'', Yamauchi Tadayoshi, contributing the ''[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]]'' tower gate in 1631 and a drum tower in 1649. Following the Meiji restoration, the shrine was renamed Tosa Jinja in 1871 and it was designated a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|''Kokuhei-chusha''}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], it was redesignated a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines|Jinja Honchō]]. In 2003, an [[archaeological excavation]] on the west side of the precincts found the foundations of the medieval mansion and other structures related to temples and shrines. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *{{nihongo|'''Honden'''|本殿||}}, built by Chōsokabe Motochika, and completed in 1571. It is a five by four bay structure with a gabled and thatched roof. The exterior is painted in rich colors and carved everywhere, and the interior is divided into inner and outer sanctuaries.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3414|title= 土佐神社本殿、幣殿及び拝殿|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Honden, Heiden oyobi Haiden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *{{nihongo|'''Heiden'''|幣殿||}} and {{nihongo|'''Haiden'''|拝殿||}}, also built by Chōsokabe Motochika, and completed in 1571. The buildings are arranged so as to represent the shape of a dragonfly flying into the main shrine, and this layout unique to Tosa Shrine. It is said to symbolize a triumphant return from battle. *{{nihongo|'''Drum Tower'''|鼓楼|| Korō}},located southeast of the Heiden. It was built in 1649 in the early Edo period by Yamauchi Tadayoshi. It is a two-story structure with a gabled and thatched roof. The upper floor has carvings and pillars painted with colors, and a drum is hung inside to tell the time.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3416|title=土佐神社鼓楼|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *{{nihongo|'''Rōmon'''|楼門 ||}}, also known as the 'Shinkomon,' it was built by Yamauchi Tadayoshi in 1631 in the early Edo period. It is a two-storied gate with three girder rows and two spans between beams (a two-storied gate with no eaves between the first and upper floors), and the roof is gabled and covered with copper. The first floor enshrines they [[:en:Zuijin|Zuijin]] between the left and right sides. The upper floor is surrounded by balustrades. The whole building is made of bare wood with almost no decoration.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3417|title=土佐神社楼門|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} Tosa-jinja honden.JPG|Honden Tosa-jinja haiden-4.jpg|Haiden Tosa-jinja10s3872.jpg|Heiden Tosa-jinja korou-3.jpg|Drum Tower Tosazinzya01.jpg|Romon ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==Notes== {{reflist|1}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). [https://books.google.com/books?id=_6rXAAAAMAAJ&q=Visiting+famous+shrines+in+japan ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{commons Category-inline|Tosa-jinja}} *[https://tosajinja.com Official home page] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] }} [[en:Tosa Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tosa-jinja}} [[eu:Tosa jinja]] [[fr:Tosa-jinja]] [[ja:土佐神社]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]] [[zh:土佐神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}}

It is a two-storied gate with three girder rows and two spans between beams (a two-storied gate with no eaves between the first and upper floors), and the roof is gabled and covered with copper. The first floor enshrines they [[:en:Zuijin|Zuijin]] between the left and right sides. The upper floor is surrounded by balustrades. The whole building is made of bare wood with almost no decoration.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3417|title=土佐神社楼門|trans-title=Tosa Jinja Korō|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} Tosa-jinja honden.JPG|Honden Tosa-jinja haiden-4.jpg|Haiden Tosa-jinja10s3872.jpg|Heiden Tosa-jinja korou-3.jpg|Drum Tower Tosazinzya01.jpg|Romon ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==Notes== {{reflist|1}} ==References== {{refbegin}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1964). [https://books.google.com/books?id=_6rXAAAAMAAJ&q=Visiting+famous+shrines+in+japan ''Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby-Fane Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1030156}} {{refend}} ==External links== {{commons Category-inline|Tosa-jinja}} *[https://tosajinja.com Official home page] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] }} [[en:Tosa Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tosa-jinja}} [[eu:Tosa jinja]] [[fr:Tosa-jinja]] [[ja:土佐神社]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Tosa Shrine]] [[zh:土佐神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q704890}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]
